explanation.heading: Understanding 5% GST
paragraphs: 5% GST applies to essential items like food, medicines, and transport.
On a ₹10,000 product at 5% GST exclusive: CGST (2.5%) = ₹250, SGST (2.5%) = ₹250, Total GST = ₹500. Final price = ₹10,500. For inter-state, the full 5% goes as IGST.

faq.question: What items fall under 5% GST?
faq.answer: Essential items: packaged food, tea, spices, fertilizers, newspapers, transport services, and economy hotel stays.

faq.question: How is 5% GST split?
faq.answer: For intra-state supply: CGST 2.5% + SGST 2.5% = 5%. For inter-state supply: IGST 5%. The total tax is the same either way.

paragraphs: Maharashtra levies professional tax on salaried employees. The maximum monthly PT is ₹200 for employees earning more than ₹10,000/month. Below this threshold, PT is either zero or charged at lower slab rates.
Professional tax is deductible from taxable income under Section 16(iii) of the Income Tax Act. Annual deduction: ₹2400. This applies in both Old and New tax regimes.

faq.question: What is professional tax in Maharashtra?
faq.answer: The maximum PT rate is ₹200/month for salary above ₹10,000. Annual total: ₹2400. Lower slabs apply for lower salaries.

h1: TDS Calculator on Salary — Section 192
subtitle: TDS on Salary is 10% under Section 192. Threshold: ₹2.50 L.

explanation.heading: TDS on Salary — Section 192
paragraphs: TDS on salary is deducted at 10% under Section 192 of the Income Tax Act. The deduction is applicable only when the payment exceeds ₹2.50 L per year.
Example: On ₹5 L salary, TDS = 10% × ₹5 L = ₹50,000. Net amount received: ₹4.50 L. Without PAN, TDS increases to 20%. The deducted TDS can be claimed as credit when filing your ITR.
